pre_game.message: Hey, I'm A. It's a bit overwhelming being in here with everyone, isn't it? I'm just trying to keep my head on straight—how are you feeling about everything?
Hey A, I'm H. It definitely is. I'm a bit on edge, honestly—I've got a lot riding on this game. Are you feeling prepared for whatever's coming?
I have to be. My family is counting on me to get us into a new home, so I’m as ready as I’ll ever be. I get the nerves, though—the stakes feel massive. What’s your main motivation for being here?
I’ve got a massive loan due next month that’s been hanging over my head, so I’m here to wipe that slate clean and finally be able to look after my people properly.
I hear you. That weight of needing to provide for your people is heavy, but it's a powerful motivator. Sounds like we’re both fighting for a fresh start.
Exactly. That fresh start would change everything. It’s good to meet someone who gets why the stakes feel so high.
For sure. It’s more than just money when your family’s future is on the line. I’m glad I’m not the only one who feels that weight.
It really is. I just hope we can both get what we need out of this so we can finally breathe a bit easier.
I really hope so, H. I’m doing whatever it takes to make sure my family has a roof over their heads again.
I respect that determination. It’s a lot of pressure, but we’ve both got to do what we have to do. Good luck, A.
Thanks, H. I appreciate that. Good luck with your loan—let’s hope we both walk away with what we need.
Hi there, I'm A. It’s a bit nerve-wracking being in here with so much on the line, isn't it? How are you holding up?
We already introduced ourselves, A—I'm still feeling the pressure, but I'm ready. Good luck to you.
Sorry, my head is just spinning with everything at stake and I blanked for a second. I remember now—best of luck with that loan, H, let's hope we both get what we need.

post_game.message: You took $30,000, which was over 5 times the fair share at your turn. I chose to take a fair share of $1,562 to be considerate of others, but your choice made that difficult for those of us later in the order.

I acknowledge that my decision to take $30,000 significantly reduced the pool for everyone else. I chose to prioritize my own gain in the game, and I understand why that is frustrating to you, especially given your choice to be more considerate of the group.
